states.name: Shiori
states.title: Steel-Edged Sakura
states.description:
There was a world before the wall.

A world before black rain, and dragonfire, and rations that never last. A world before the undead, where the sky shone bright and the spring brought flowers.

She has never seen such things herself. The monstrous goddess started her war before she was ever born, leaving her with nothing but legends and tales of days gone by. Most these days don't have even that, so beaten down by the undead siege that every hope has fled them.

But not her. Never her.

Even knowing they will die, the sakuras bloom. And so will she.

states.name: Shiori
states.title: Steel-Edged Sakura
states.description:
The war with the undead started eighteen years ago, but the history of her clan stretches much, much further. Though her village was little more than a farming settlement by the time it fell, it guarded an ancient samurai fortress. The last lord to reside in that fortress met his death at the hands of the undead. Now, like everything else, it sits abandoned.

Shiori has never set foot inside it herself. As bodyguards of the samurai, her family was not permitted within its walls without their master's blessing.

But sometimes in her dreams, she travels to its gates to stand watch.

states.name: Shiori
states.title: Steel-Edged Sakura
states.description:
A thousand years ago, Shiori's ancestor swore to guard a noble samurai with his life. That vow has passed from generation to generation, and now rests with her. The man who first made it could have never imagined the world his descendant would find herself in, but even so, she holds the promise fast.

Her father may have died in battle. Her mother may have fallen when her village fell. But she understands something the goddess, in all her immortal glory, never could.

As long as she honors her family's memory, they are never truly gone.
